Former Love Islander 'contestant' has made claims suggesting the popular ITV2 show staged.
Well, it's not actually that shocking, surely?
The claims come from last series' contestant Tyla Carr, who says producers dictate what conversations are had the show.
What viewers don't see there always producer site.
They don't live the villa with but someone generally comes every hour have chat.
They would often tannoy announcement asking for someone come the sofa have chat with one the producers.
They tell you what they want you talk about, and who with.
Tyla also said the contestants are told they 'must tell producers' they're going having important chat with someone else. That way they can ensure the microphones are there 'pick the confrontation'.
Apparently they forget inform the crew their 'important conversations', stars are forced film the conversation again.
Liv Attwood had dump Sam Gowland twice last year, which was embarrassing for both them.
Love Island spokespeople have denied the claims.
absolutely untrue suggest Love Island fake. The opinions they have and the relationships formed are completely within the control the Islanders themselves.
have said since series one, Love Island combination reality and produced elements. Any produced elements are designed allow viewers understand what the Islanders are feeling and help move narrative threads on.
Tonight's (July episode set bring some drama for Georgia Steel, whose Love Island partner Josh Denzel looks set get with Kazimir Crossley.
ITV2 preview clip the end Friday's (June 29) episode, Josh was seen kissing Kaz they shared bed together the villa.
Georgia had made the decision stay away from the new contestants and slept outside day bed with Dani Dyer, she said she saw 'future' with Josh.
Call old-fashioned all you like, but behaviour like this just one the many, many reasons cannot stand the programme.
Viewers were outraged the clip.
The producers love island need send Georgia warning text about Josh because swear god she's dumped from the island because she stayed loyal I'm going cry she's too precious.
Not vibing with how disloyal the majority the #LoveIsland boys are this year This why many girls have many issues from behaviour like this and heart going BREAK when Josh chooses his new girl over Georgia when she's been loyal. can't deal with it. [sic].
 can't deal with
Love Island ITV2 Sunday Friday 9pm.

What device are you reading this on? Or, better still, how many devices could you choose from? Probably quite few.
Connected devices are multiplying, and that's one the major challenges every team your business has deal with every day. More devices means more interactions with single customer, and more data about those interactions.
The trouble is: everything seems connected except your departments. Your Sales and Service teams might work the same floor, but they're working silos, they might well different planets.
That's when customer data complexity can turn into chaos but doesn't have to.
Think Amazon isn't your competitor? Think again. Slick customer experiences raise the bar for everyone: Once consumers get used great ecommerce experience, they won't put with sloppy one from their bank.
Faster service that gives them the outcome they want, first time.
Smarter service your reps have the right information front them.
Connected experience that pulls all relevant data from all channels, even before your service agent picks the phone.
Service anytime, anywhere whenever the customer needs it, however they want it.
how you give your reps Sales and Service the power deliver these expectations?
It's not easy ask, especially when you factor the fact that 70% companies have not tied their Sales and Service efforts together.
But it's worth the effort: we've all heard the statistic that says 80% your sales come from 20% your existing customers, but did you know that repeat customers spend 30% more than new customers? And that people are 86% more likely buy from you again they had positive customer experience?
What does connected sales and service look like the real-world?
When you connect Sales and Service, you can three things really well.
Get personal. With single, 360-degree view the customer, you can engage them across any channel they choose use, with the right message action the right time.
Enjoy the 360-degree view. Take the blinkers off your Sales and Service people and the benefits are mutual. Your salespeople can sell better when they know what's going with customer's case history, service reps can identify cross- and up-sell opportunities and identify VIP customers, and managers can view the health the business across both realms.
Salesforce World Tour London 2017, heard from brands like Coca-Cola, who are developing new generation sales and service professionals without wall separating their departments. Both sides (though shouldn't call them sides any more) are trained slip seamlessly from service queries sales opportunities and vice versa.
We're big fans the work the Hive brand have been doing with the help the Salesforce platform create single, coherent journey for their connected home customers from purchase installation, and service and beyond.
You can learn more about the story here, but read hear from two the people making all work. Neil Procter, Global Head Customer Systems, and Terence Sorrell, Business Solutions Manager Centrica Connected Home, talked about Hive's connected customer journeys.
The Hive products that make Centrica Connected Home were conceived help consumers take control their home environment easily, making everything from their thermostat their lighting flexible, smart and, most importantly, connected.
"And when we're talking British Gas customer, for example, Salesforce helps automate the co-branding all our content when need to," Terence added.
Ready start creating those great customer experiences? Download this insightful and practical Creating Connected Customer Experience guide.

you want understand the future, just look what rich people today.
Peter Drucker said that "to defend yesterday larger risk than create tomorrow." Concentrating for too long what was the expense what will has been the formula for failure for many CEOs.
What you are trying develop manager kind inductive skill reading the terrain; knowing intuitively when the paradigms are about change bust endure.
possible honor the past and the same time make real the failings that lead want better tomorrow.
[Economics and technology] are the wrong places begin. The fundamental changes are social, and they are the greatest changes imaginable.
The future already here just not very evenly distributed.
How can you make sense the future when you only have data about the past? That's the role theory, look into the future.
...in the long term the most important question for company not what you are but what you are becoming.
Wise men say, and not without reason, that whoever wishes foresee the future must consult the past; for human events ever resemble those preceding times. This arises from the fact that they are produced men who have been, and ever will be, animated the same passions and thus they must necessarily have the same results.
The problem and the essence what makes forecasting hard that human nature hardwired abhor uncertainty. are fascinated change, but our effort avoid uncertainty either dismiss outliers entirely attempt turn them into certainties that they are not.

Some advaitins claim that Nirguna Brahman spoke through Krishna, but this accordance with advaitin theory that Brahman without any characteristic, speaking not considered characteristic, act independent individual? According advaita.
According Advaita, there only Brahman, and nothing more. Everything you see, the world, just Brahman.
The scriture [atharva veda] says this world Brahman. All Brahman. Therefore, all this world nothing but Brahman. What you impute thing [superimpose] not separate from that which you impute [the substratum]. The basis all Brahman. Therefore, what you call the world not the world, but Brahman.
So, Sri Krishna and everyone else are Brahman. When Sri Krishna speaks only Brahman who speaks. But also when little ant die, just Brahman who die.
And further, whatsoever the seed all existences that Arjuna; nor there anything, moving unmoving that can exist without Me.
When you see Brahman little insect with legs, etc, you call "ant Brahman" (usually without the Brahman part, just ant When you can put qualities something, that saguna. And Brahman everything, everything manifestation saguna Brahman.
But, order realize that, pay special attention the part", which are sages, jivanmuktas, avataras, devas, srutis, shanti, cit, etc. And usually call saguna Brahman only that part (and leave ant, and everything else, out the term, only help understanding and realization).
When you think about Brahman without upadhis only (without maya), without any quality that you can think of, that kind sunya (void, which actually not void because full Brahman), that you call nirguna Brahman. So, nirguna Brahman cannot speak, because does, then call saguna Brahman.
But, Brahman alone is, the only one who speaks, and listen and does everything.
For him the act offering Brahman, the oblation Brahman. Brahman offered into the fire Brahman. Brahman that which attained him who realizes Brahman his works.

I'm almost there with the first book the trilogy... I'm the middle line edits and crutch-word slaying and then will off professional editor find all the bits that still need fixing.
was feeling especially pleased with myself just before started this, managed create macro Word that would, with couple clicks, highlight all over-used words the ms. I'm not saying managed create the macro the first time tried (or even the second, third, fourth...) but did eventually make (and even made 'How To' sheet send out couple writer friends that they can create their own versions).
Crutch-words are words that author relies and uses far too often. know what most mine are (though maybe some new ones have crept in). spot them I'm going through edits, try and kill them off, but inevitably, hundreds the little critters still make through the line-edits. What macro does change all the crutch-words (that know of) into the same word but with yellow highlighter it. I've then printed off the whole (and got umpteen paper-cuts I've stacked the pages neatly) with the words highlighted.
Next going over every word the and tightening all much can. used hate this stage, but now love it. It's bit depressing how much yellow currently each page, but it's better kill off those over-used words now, than leave them in.
It's slow process. can't more than short section one sitting find brain stops working improving all and just reads it. I'm using minute sand-timer and then getting and walk/doing something else before going back it. The print-out 268 pages long and the only way can tackle things like this (without going doolally) split into small chunks.
have say, I'm excited have reached this stage with it!

has recently come attention that not everyone using AutoCAD the same way. cases like this any way that not 'my way' automatically considered the 'wrong way' but this time think 'my way' and the 'right way' are the same thing. This past week was using the Maptek Vulcan menu option: File Import AutoCAD Design Strings (dxf) import dxf file from reputable consulting firm. have imported lots dxf files career and never had any problem with them. Imagine confusion, then, when this file imported the completely wrong space.
Sometimes data comes into Maptek Vulcan the wrong coordinate units and necessary use the View Window Fit Layer... option see the data screen. familiar with this error but this data was data and drawn MINE COORDINATES. What was the problem? was even more confused when the data imported back into AutoCAD the correct units.
The coordinates Vulcan were much larger than the mine coordinates should have been, about times larger. After some back and forth with the consulting company finally came light that they all their drafting inches. The fix, after getting this information, was simple scaling the dxf file data but INCHES? Who designs inches?
Alright, understand that AutoCAD was originally created for mechanical engineers and that drawings for machined parts could, legitimately, drawn inches. But aren't the machined parts business and neither are the mining consultants. can't believe that anyone would designing mine plans anything other than feet meters (maybe yards you work coal). Inches are right out!
The other thing that bothers here that AutoCAD recognizes the coordinate units inches and will convert them back feet for you. Why doesn't Maptek Vulcan this? The coordinate units are flag the dxf file. think this something $50,000 software should take into account.
requested upgrade logged for this improvement the Mine Engineers Maptek. haven't received JIRA reference number for this yet but will update this post when (if?) arrives.

couple weekends ago spoke youth rally Centerville. The theme was the cross and each speaker was asked share something about its meaning. spoke the idea that the cross about grace not guilt.
Too many times seems that our main focus the atonement guilt. sin nailed Jesus the cross. sin caused his suffering. sin caused his pain. sin caused his death. get what folks are saying here but sometimes feels like the cross becomes more about what we've done than what God's done.
The focus love. Christ didn't die heap more guilt upon us. died remove guilt. died, the writer Hebrews said, "to cleanse from guilty conscience." When look upon the cross should reminded God's grace more than our guilt.
This not what many were taught. were taught see the cross reminder sin. actually reminder forgiven sin. Our sin more. has been removed. This was driven home for moving (and unintentional) way the end the youth rally.
The church had erected cross the stage. They laid out pens and Post-It notes nearby asking everyone come up, write down their sins and stick them the cross.
I'd seen this done dozens times before but this one didn't exactly planned. The Post-It notes weren't sticky enough. They wouldn't stay. They kept falling off. The students would back and re-stick them but they'd stubbornly flutter the ground.
While the youth deacons panicked, saw something beautiful the failed illustration. The sins wouldn't stick. matter how hard tried they just wouldn't stay. Why not? Because they are forgiven. They are more. They have been removed the power the cross.
It's not about condemnation. It's about salvation.
It's not about failure. It's about victory.
It's not about shame. It's about love.
And it's not about guilt. It's about grace.

She has asthma. And sometimes could get really bad. Especially when she was very nervous and scared. When she was little girl, bullies would tease her because it. have too much time think," she said herself. Her doctor ordered her stay bed (she felt something that didn't feel right) and she had many days would take not moving look forward.

Sometimes the world can feel like dark and scary place, and can all too easy forget that there are good things happening all around every single day. Which exactly why we're crying happy tears over the story Skye 3-year-old cancer survivor who just served flower girl her bone marrow donor's wedding.
We're not crying you are!
Skye was diagnosed with rare form leukemia when she was less than year old. Doctors gave her less than 10% chance survival and told her family she would need bone marrow transplant soon possible. And that's where Hayden Hatfield Ryals Alabama comes in.
Hayden, 26, had registered become bone marrow donor through the nonprofit the Match when she was college. "It's the easiest thing. It's cheek swab swab your cheek, put envelope and they send off...I thought there's like one gazillion chance that would ever match," she said June interview with the Dothan Eagle.
But she WAS match, and with baby Skye. Hayden didn't hesitate when she found out she could help save life (even though donations are all completely anonymous, she didn't know whose life would be). Shortly after she donated, she sent anonymous letter through the Match Skye's family, letting them know she was thinking them. Skye's mom, Talia sent anonymous letter back, and from there they decided connect via email, calls, and texts.
Skye's mom went say, look forward the years come with Skye growing and continuing this relationship with Hayden, and watching Hayden grow up, too."
And perhaps the best part this whole story? Little Skye currently remission.
you're interested registering become bone marrow donor and potentially saving life, you can visit the the Match website for more information.

This week, Alex Irizarry and Jean Hyppolite came Million Cups tell about SmartStop, the first product their company, LITA.
The idea for SmartStop came about from Alex and Jean's observations children waiting for the bus stop every morning often for 20-30 minutes. "In 2017, why there nothing these kids can track the bus?" They wondered. And what the child misses their bus? Parents have way know, they realized. "That's why team and created SmartStop," Jean said.
After researching and looking, Alex and Jean could not find product that allows parents and kids track the school bus. With some luck, they found SocialCubix, "one the leading app makers the country," who became their official investor and partner.
SmartStop 3-in-1 mobile platform that allows the users track their school bus through Google live-tracking." The app simple use for parents, students, bus drivers, and schools.
The school sends all parents assigned security code and invite add their children the SmartStop app. Once the invite accepted, parents have the ability track their students and the child's specific school bus. Parents receive notifications through the app that allow them see when the child arrives the bus stop, picked (or misses the bus), and arrives school. the end the day, similar notifications let parents know when the child departs school and arrives back the bus stop.
Students are able access the app through invite from their parent. Once they've accepted the invite, they can set their own personal profile and track their designated bus. they miss the bus for any reason, the SmartStop app can reroute them the nearest bus stop, they still make school.
Both parents and students receive notifications when substitute bus driver operating the route. also lets them know any changes the bus its route.
School bus drivers also receive security code from the school district, which allows them access the SmartStop app and create personal profile. Drivers start the app their phone before leaving and tap button "Start Route." Then they put their phone away and the SmartStop app runs the background, tracking the bus along the route.
Alex and Jean told the Million Cups audience that they are still seeking funding for SmartStop. "If you guys can lend hand and let know what companies are here locally, really want start locally first and grow from there," Alex said.

BMW started 1916, stands for Bayerische Motoren Werke, when translated into English means Bavarian Motor Works. Initially, BMW mostly made airplane engines, until they started making cars that set records for speed. Today, BMW's are still known the ultimate driving machines and have built respectable performance car brand.
You can quickly identify the brand with its blue and white checkered circle logo every car. They took off during the 1980s through the 1990s. 1996 the model was massive success after served James Bond's car the movie GoldenEye. BMW kept the momentum the victory and made hardtop version with unique design called the Z3M. The Z3M was very powerful and sporty yet practical.
BMW has history making classic cars, but what makes car classic? Most people would say the brand and the long line history that made the brand. Car fans enjoy the lineage vehicles the brand has made, and the essence what stands for BMW has turned some them into cult-like classics (like 2002!). The BMW's Series Coupe became classic part because how rare and now worth more than when originally produced.
BMW cars are different than classic Corvette Mustang and are more sought after collectors. Another car that considered BMW classic the E92 M3. It's the only ever have engine which revs 8,250 RPM. BMW launched its first electric car, i3, 2014. Right now may not seem like classic, but few years, just might be.
BMW cars share some similarities with each other, the small footprint, they are lightweight and have pure focus steering. this day the still considered BMWs greatest car made. was designed Giugiaro, well known Italian automobile designer, and the BMW quickly became legendary.
Lastly, the E39 540i gentleman's sporting sedan that could easily beat the Mustang any drag race. has whopping 400 horsepower that can still outrace majority new cars.
BMW has endured many challenges since their inception, but they are masters adjusting and staying true their vision and mission. Their innovation, customer loyalty, and unmatched driving pleasure have led their reputation being the ultimate driving car and collector's dream. Not all BMWs are classics, yet. majority the models will become classics their own time. you require the ultimate driving experience, then BMW the car for you.

The UK-based band Pirates Panama chatted with Digital Journal about their new music, their forthcoming EP, well their musical influences.
their song "The Chase," they said, "It started out with two chords played back and forth which really got our feet stomping which made think this can become something! then added the guitar intro part and jammed out, the whole song came together just few hours. The lyrics are about chasing our dreams and getting out the rut were the time."
They noted that their song "Been There Before" sounded quite bit different first. "We had cool drum intro and lots layers building but decided strip back for the record head straight into the song! The original guitar riff has stayed the same since day one! The idea was something that I've played around with for years and eventually thought this could really something! like mix this track when play live and extend the intro and have bit fun with the arrangements."
their music inspirations, they said, "We can't pin point one single source inspiration for our music. usually just comes naturally. course have our influences but are always taking inspiration from really bizarre sources such things read online story we've been told the pub."
Regarding the origin their band's name, they said, "The name came about after good few days throwing ideas around until got from night out and started talking about how cool pirates were and then suddenly came with Pirates (blank). Then, all had think and came with Panama! The next morning all said Pirates Panama, and then the name was born."
Their future plans including releasing their new EP, well continuing your across America and Europe. "We are working some cool shows which will announced soon," they revealed.
the digital transformation the music industry, they said, "Live streaming has become really big thing across all platforms now and has really blown up, remember when started using twitcam while ago and how much hassle was share all the links and get people visit the page, now with Facebook, you can instantly stream. It's crazy! This has really helped artists interact with their fans."
Regarding their daily use technology, they said, "We usually use our Smart phones every single day for most aspects the band. From social media posting photos and laying down acoustic demos vocals. matter where are can always grab our phones and write lyrics record quick ideas. It's crazy that using just our phones can record ideas, Skype our team, live broadcasts and even create artwork for shows."
For their fans, they said, "Thank you all you pirates out there old and new for the continued support. are working hard produce new music for you guys and sorting out shows. Can't wait see you the road soon."

Are your goals S.M.A.R.T.? Start the New Year Right!
When discovered this, few years ago, changed life! that point was simply writing down "what want" and, must admit, suffered from lot "vague" goals like, "make more money", "exercise more"... and, well, was barely reaching any goals. Then found the S.M.A.R.T. method and looked over goals from the past and realized that those goals "got" were ones that "accidentally" met the S.M.A.R.T criteria.
While that famous Harvard study about written goals has been debunked, find that writing out your goals adds incredible sense power and commitment the process. Write down your goals and then review them often! Nothing more exciting than "checking off" your list goal that you've achieved!
how you create S.M.A.R.T. goal?
Specific Take look these two goals: want lose weight" want lose 35lbs June 15th". you read them, which elicits emotional response you? Does the want lose weight" anything for you? too vague, too uncommitted? the other hand, you read want lose 35lbs June 15th" chances are you feel bit motivated. You are able create picture your goal achieved and you may even feel the excitement feeling 35lbs lighter your body. You want it.
Measureable The question ask yourself here "How will know when have achieved this goal". How will you feel, what, specifically will have changed? number the scale? specific number your paycheck? Your first online sale? Holding the keys your new home car?
Attainable this goal realistic? great goal JUST out reach. Now that does not mean you can't have vision something much grander, but you look that grand vision, create stepping stones get there- the form smaller, realistic goals reach for.
Attainable also means something that YOU are charge of. Setting goal put new roof the house July 10th -when your spouse insists they themselves and they don't seem too interested doing makes the attainability this goal less likely. Keep your goals within your power.
Relevant This area where work very closely with clients. There lot involved this process, but the short answer here is: Ask yourself how does this goal fit your life? For example it's important for you parent who attends almost every one your child's sports games, plays, school events, etc, does goal making $600,000 year the road sales rep goal become Broadway actor fit into your overall vision for your life? lot subtleties come play with Relevancy, the very least look each goal from that broader life view.
Time-Bound this what they call goal with deadline". you don't set "end" date, date that you plan have goal by, it's easy just take your time getting there, cast the goal aside when the going gets bit rough. you're goal worded something like this "Someday I'll..." then you're setting yourself for long road the goal.
Use this criteria create some great goals. Goals that are clear and motivating. You can also check out article Goals Vision matters for more details.
Using the S.M.A.R.T. method create 3-5 goals within each category.
Write these goals down simple sentence. Remember include specifics and "achieve by" date.

turned around Stanford, IN, and headed back along different roads. Again brief flat section.

passed couple quarries.
the crow flies, was never more than about eight miles from home. There was great variety scenery, for such short distance. The ride totaled around miles, but felt like more they were tough miles.

Joey was the most affectionate, loyal, loving, playful, nurturing and happy little dog I've ever known. discovered him through Wire Fox Terrier rescue agency back 2008 that specializes finding forever homes for dogs. have doubt that Higher Power was responsible for joining Joey with our family, because never even met him before deciding adopting him.
was already and half years old when joined our family, but from the moment met him bring him home, was showering father with kisses was truly match made Heaven.
family and know how blessed were have had nine amazing years with him, but any pet owner knows, matter how long you have with your beloved pet never seems long enough. Joey lived 13.5 years, incredibly, near-perfect health. But the early morning hours May 17th, 2017, had what later learned was stroke and parents made the difficult, but selfless decision put him sleep that same afternoon.
And, very sadly regrettably, wasn't able there when took his last breaths because had undergone invasive brain surgery the day prior, 5/16/17, and therefore was hospitalized when fell ill and when gained his angel wings. that oddly coincidental irony that breaks heart the most; don't feel any sense closure since wasn't able say "goodbye".
But, I'm very proud this video that feel perfectly sums the amazing companionship and love that all had with little Joey. I'd honored for anyone watch it. Thank you very much advance.

Despite the increased publicity over the dangers texting while driving, many teenagers and their parents have yet get the message.
According report issued Pew Research, fully one-third cell phone users aged and admitted texting while driving. Among passengers, percent teens said they have been car while the driver was texting, and per cent have been car when the driver used cell phone way that put everyone danger.
focus groups, teens reported variety motivations for texting while driving, including the need report their whereabouts friends and parents, getting directions, and flirting with significant others.
According the latest research from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, 2008 there were 5,870 fatalities and estimated 515,000 people injured crashes where least one form driver distraction was reported. Young drivers are particular concern, the highest incidence distracted driving occurs the under-20 age group.
Although the report only surveyed teens 12-17, adults were clearly part the larger problem. "My mom, sister and brother will sit behind the wheel the whole time and just text away," reported one middle school-aged boy. "My dad drives like he's drunk, said tenth grader. "His phone just like sitting right front his face and puts his knees the bottom the steering wheel and tries text."
And texting not the only culprit when comes distracted driving. Global Positioning Systems are also responsible for taking driver's eyes off the road, particularly when they are accessed via smartphones other handheld devices.
Despite the wide publicity given distracted driver accidents and studies that highlight the dangers, many teens remain almost nonchalant about the risks. "It really doesn't bother me," wrote one high school boy. "I've made and received calls almost every time I've driven."
When came being risk passenger, teens had variety responses. Some were angry about being endangered. Others were less concerned. worry about they can it," said one boy. usually watch the road when happens and tell them they're going off the road something. don't really care though."
Life more precious than reading text message. Mobile application from drivesafe.ly helps you concentrate the road while making sure you are connected.

MUMBAI: student's life can hard exam pressures, the stress competition, parents with lofty dreams breathing down your neck perform well least better than Sharmaji ladka who got into the best college town! With all these things juggle, some students also have deal with the extra pressure dealing with new place and people when they move new city for studies like the protagonist Vaibhav (Mayur More) TVF Originals upcoming series Kota Factory.
Anyone who moves city like Kota, any other city for studies the biggest issue that plagues them that feeling being outsider. Everything alien. From the place itself, the people, the way they function and vast country like India, sometimes even the language can make you feel like outsider.
Most students who move other cities have deal with either hostel food have eat out most the times. such moments, all one can think about all those times when mom would serve hot food meal times even the times when you were studying late the night and there she would with plate fruit and tea help you study during the night before exams!
This one's especially brutal. Most students who are exceptionally good and toppers their own schools might not actually the best when they new city. new city means more people, more toppers and perhaps someone who way smarter than you!
Little wonder that most students who move new cities end bringing their laundry home lot times just prefer live messy rooms because there's one clean after them! mom, maid, one! Most people think helps build character doing things yourself, but the reality is, with hour study sessions, you really blame students for not wanting chores?
Most students move new cities study when they're just out school. Most 16-18 year olds have never lived away from their families had friends outside their schools. leave all this behind and into the unknown can unnerving and honestly, little scary. And the homesickness can crushing!
But, the great Baazigar said 'Kuch Paane Liye, Kuch Khona Padta Hai' and that's where the fun lies! Moving new city for studies can daunting. But once you settle you start looking the world with fresh perspective and start enjoying yourself. Treat like adventure and you'll have fun exploring new place and making new friends and perhaps also find time study hard enough get into that dream college!

It's that time year, when companies have started release their Christmas items and love it! I'll honest, last year went bit over the top with the Christmas release purchases, but this year I've decided only stick items that truly catch eye, and the Galaxy Palette Rihanna's Fenty Beauty range definitely did that.
The Galaxy Palette limited edition palette release, which contains 'stardusted' shades, each with different finish either sheer, glittery topcoats, smokey shimmery hues with more pigmentation their base colour. I'll say now this palette does not contain ANY matte shades, you are one not like palette without mattes, then this isn't for you. Also note that this all glitter palette too, but with different glitter formulas again this isn't your type thing, then you may want stay away.
personally have issues with the palette being all glitter. Let's honest here, it's nothing new the market have all glitter palette (Moondust Urban Decay ring bell?), when saw that Rihanna had decided venture down this route thought 'good her'. really think about it, the market saturated with mattes mixture mattes and shimmers, it's great see something different, not necessarily new.
Now course when working with glitters you will have work your application, and this palette exception. Most the shadows work best with glitter glue ensure they last long time your eyelids, otherwise you'll experience some fall out throughout the day well some fading (especially from the glitter topcoats).
The shadows with more pigmentation worked better and found that didn't necessarily need use glitter glue even base colour for them show (Lightyear, Sunburst and Midnight Bolt). Some the more interesting shades such Space Owt and Ultraviolit, found, had quite subtle pigmentation, they would definitely need placed top base eyeshadow. Another point note that most these shades work best when applying with your fingers, it's easier pack the glitters with less fall out.
The packaging the Galaxy Palette real standout. LOVE the rainbow reflective packaging the palette itself, although does attract finger print stains! The palette's outer packaging comes with the Fenty name written glitter! Overall I've enjoyed using this palette something for reach for everyday? No. Will repurchase this? But I'll definitely making the most during this party season. This palette has short shelf life months, which may because the glitters.
The Galaxy Palette retails for £39, and can found in-store and online Harvey Nichols here. For more palette reviews click here.

Yesterday, experienced happiness degree that could hardly ever surpassed. was the height happiness. fact, was happy hurt.
every fall, went the Fryeburg Fair Fryeburg, Maine which pretty much the granddaddy Maine fairs, featuring more agriculture than any other fair the state. was glorious day. The weather was perfect fall weather sunny and the high 50s.
But about halfway through the afternoon, stumbled upon bliss somewhat unexpectedly. had just found our way into the section that had all the kids' rides and had purchased pass that would grant access for our two oldest children any ride. brought Camden and Acadia around the various rides that enticed them, mother volunteered take Winslow too young and too short any the rides into the little toddler section, where she could ride little see-saws and push around little cars.

This not unique experience me. Every parent has experienced it. Sometimes routinely. Indeed, even though this particular moment seemed heightened moment ecstasy for for whatever reason, enjoy such moments every once-in-a-while observe the happiness children.
The truth is, the greatest happiness person can experience seeing someone else happy. It's not rocket science. Thaipusam 2012 Singapore, set Flickr.
Until saw these people barefoot with giant fish hooks, skewers and other metal spikes several feet long piercing their bodies and hauling large heavy structures, never would have imagined this practice still being observed, especially, such developed country.
Hindus subject themselves painful rituals (piercing themselves with hooks, skewers and small lances) honor the Thaipusam festival Singapore and Malaysia.
The colorful celebration period both abstinence and fasting, and observed day thanksgiving and paying penance commemoration the day when the Hindu Goddess Pavarthi gave her son Lord Muruga the god war and fertility invincible lance with which destroyed evil demons. (Wikipedia).
Hindus believe they are cleansed all sins and their misdeeds can redeemed observing these strict rituals and prayers during Thaipusam. Some devotees carrying heavy ornate structures called kavadis they walk barefoot, while others have their tongues, cheeks and backs pierced.
were watching from the end the route, after the devotees had gone through the Tank Road Temple with their offerings. So, many had already disconnected their carriages that they had been pulling and few had removed some hooks.
Seeing the hooks and skewers removed looked more painful than seeing them attached the body. Ready collapse from the pain they are pulled out, one one.
These devotees walked over 4km this morning enduring the pain, some still dancing around the completion. Others, gesturing for support from their followers, they walked into the final tent for removal the piercings.
They can not talk with the large skewers through their cheeks and mouth, they are surrounded others closely observing, case help needed.
kept our distance, didn't have worry about the kids getting the way grabbing anything they shouldn't touching.
These people are dedicated, crazy, I'll let you make your own opinion!

ran the potatoes through cheese grater because don't have ricer. That got them into super aerated pile. added the egg and added flour until the whole thing was clinging together. lifted onto floured surface, kneaded for about minutes. I'm not pasta adept don't know overworked safe assume did.
blanched boiling water until they floated the top, then pan fried them olive oil until golden brown both sides. They were too chewy when they came out (No doubt wrong proportions ingredients) but bizarrely, they tasted and smelt like English pancakes. I'm just wondering, there something with the egg and the flour that would cause this?
Not using ricer mill could cause you inadvertently over-work the mixture; the time you feel like you get the right tactile response could very well over-glutenous (this explains the chewy center). your budget permits, even cheap ricer small mill would help (they're found for low $10), not, just try mashing with fork until fine prior mixing with the flour and egg.
You need somewhat aggressive with salt, others have pointed out. Twice much you think you need might seem like lot, but try that general measure.
Those points could also contributors the taste you describe but the sum the ingredients matter here, too. How old was the flour? With pasta and gnocci, use brand that's clearly marked with packaged date can rotated properly.
Also, did you groove them? Little grooves the sides help the Maillard reaction they don't need fry long, which keeps the oil from going pungent, and helps hold the salt little better.

For the last years have been visiting the Gold Coast. Initially these trips were part our yearly family holiday, but now they are typically part cycling trip, mates tow.
Last year ventured ride 144k, that took through rain forest, cane fields, rolling farm land and ranches. Road side fruit stands remote areas along the ride are not uncommon which adds the beauty this adventure. decided tackle this ride once again.
started out sleepy beachside town Currumbin, 15k south Surfers Paradise and headed out along the Currumbin Creek Road toward the hills. just over 10k into the ride, left hand turn and the first climb for the day greets us.
The Tomewin Mountain Road heads towards the NSW border. Undulating roads for 9.2k, climbing ridge that rewards you with amazing scenery over the Currumbin Valley. truly breathtaking more ways than one.
QLD roads are well signed throughout the state with gradients providing all road users with information what gear use. This different for the cyclist. Our first sign; 14% for 800 meters. Thank you very much!
strong cyclist will make the NSW boarder this road about minutes. From this point the road drops down nice gentle descent with amazing views toward the NSW coast. was the bottom this descent that encountered python! Stretched across the road, making its way from one side the other. must have been close meters long. Only the QLD hinterland!
quick roll through cane fields and right hand turn the number where started the next section the ride. Rolling roads through the valley back toward QLD.
stopped for quick can soft drink Chillingham before tackling the climb back the ridge. were NSW, the roads aren't marked with how steep this climb is. But when could, would look down and see what Garmin was reading and think through the sweat dripping from brow that saw 17%.
The climb starts earnest you pass through Numinbah and approximately long.
quick rest top the border and were off again with another gentle descent toward Natural Bridge. The roads through here roll through some brilliant country side with horse ranches either side based the bottom steep intimidating mountains.
After 75k right hand turn onto Pikes Road (the Goat Track) what will guide Springbrook. sign the entry the road greats letting know that will get steep but there will also respite half way through the climb. This respite did not come! The road climbs and climbs and then climbs little bit more for just under 7k. one stage was turning the pedals with just enough power register speed 6.9k/h! the middle the day with the QLD sun beating down you, the hill seems just that little more difficult.
quick stop the top the 'Goat Track' quaint restaurant talk about our struggle and battle with the hill before continue toward the last stop before head home, 'the Best Look Out All'.
Gradients excess 10% smack the legs around more than couple occasions make our way the additional 10K the look out. times you still question whether getting off the bike and walking would acceptable?
The lookout amazing! the time very hard think too whether not all the climbing was actually worth it. reflection is!
Now with all the climbing done, all that was left was descend. Sweeping corners, smooth roads and forest either side; this brilliant 15k drop back toward the valley.
get closer the Gold Coast, the suburbs build around us, eventually completely replacing the forest landscape that leave behind. The final 20k the ride main roads. power home with nice tail wind eventually arriving back our accommodation.
summary; the QLD Hinterlands provide for great riding. The adventure that shared one will fondly look back with great satisfaction for many year come and keep wanting come back for more!

However, you are coming the U.S. using personal yacht personal boat, then you will need Visa. the other hand, you're going the U.S. cruise, then you can enter the U.S. using your ESTA.
Bear mind that ESTA does not guarantee entry the U.S., that decision dependent Customs and Border Protection (CBP). The CBP can deny cancel ESTA any point during before travel.

Those who know will know the crazy journey I've been this year.
But one the most interesting things I've noticed happen the change myself and own personality. I've always been fascinated the Myers Briggs Type Indicator personality test having been brought family who have always discussed the different types length and looked very deeply into our individuality.
For long can remember, have strongly identified INFJ (ie. Introverted iNtuitive Feeling Judging... would highly recommend having read the different types and what the words/letters themselves actually mean you have idea what I'm about!). I've written about type length and what means etc. and always believed that was permanent/fixed thing and that would this way forever that this was the essence and wouldn't change.
About month ago just had random thought take the test again, considering what has happened over the past year, and fascinatingly not one, but two letters have changed!
what does this mean about me?
This means that currently feel most energised when around other people and socialising. It's the biggest change think because before was EXTREMELY introverted (ie. felt most energised when alone and having time myself, and felt drained energy when being around others). this time life now, have larger friend group who enjoy seeing much can, and feel happiest when around lots other people crowds completely different before!
This has stayed the same; use intuition and abstract see patterns, possibilities and make decisions (rather than logic and concrete evidence).
Also stayed the same; when making decisions doing things put peoples' emotions and feelings priority rather than rationality.
This big change too! means have become more spontaneous and flexible and adaptable change suppose I've kind been forced into it!) rather than 'Judging' was before, which meant preferred structure, planning and routine.
I'm always advocate for learning about what makes you and other people around you tick, and I'd highly recommend taking this test you haven't already! It's useful for and super interesting compare with people you know.
Have you ever thought re-take your personality test?

Why this: In-house marketers and entrepreneurs have tough. Managing just one account, you're stuck your vacuum, unsure what you're seeing your campaigns normal not. agency, we're privileged position work with many eCommerce companies and have much broader perspective into what's working and what's not across variety brands.
wanted open our data bit (without sharing confidential client info) give you better idea the larger landscape.
this post, wanted 2017 annual review and take you through our numbers and results from spending several million dollars digital advertising (Facebook ads and AdWords primarily).
Last year worked with about eCommerce brands. This dashboard general rollup all those campaigns into one that can get some high-level benchmarks.
This should helpful for you comparing your numbers against our clients.
course, every business different and there's different price points and customers it's not apples apples comparison, but this will give you general idea what our numbers looked like.
Our cost per purchase overall for 2017 was $22. This very, very good acquire paying customers. 2016 were $41, cut that half!
Depending your price point and margins, $22 might terrible amazing. We've had clients who needed $15 CPA profitable and other's who were very happy with $100 CPA.
2017 ROI was over 3x, compared 2016. HOWEVER, the last year actually way better because were way more scale. spent much more money and ultimately made our clients more profit.
you're seeing about meaningful amount spend, that's normal based what we're seeing with different brands, different price points and industries.
lot people like brag and feel good about their 20x ROI, but that's almost always because they only spent few hundred dollars and mainly did retargeting. Ok, you spent $300 and made $8,000. Thats A.) Not that much money and B.) Not repeatable.
I'd rather consistently spend $80,000 month and make back $240,000.
Here there's very clear trend with Facebook and Google getting more expensive overall. CPM's nearly doubled year over year.
The overall Cost Per Click went 56%.
We're definitely seeing these networks get more expensive and we're going see that 2018 well.
You'll need get used paying more per click these platforms get more and more competitive.
Note for the above, this for link clicks, not ALL clicks. CTR for all clicks normally around 2-4%. CTRs went actually which promising. This shows that ads are getting more engagement.
should actually see this trend continue with the new updates Facebook announced with changes the newsfeed.
Content and ads should get more engagement now that Facebook will filtering out more and more organic branded content.
This means that for 100 people going site, pulled out their credit card and bought something.
Hopefully you're least conversion rate. Any less than that and it's tough scale and make the math work for paid traffic. But you're the 2-5% range, you should doing pretty well.
This big jump last year partially result agency getting better targeting and also Facebook improving features like Lookalikes.
take look your metrics and see how they compare this!
send out our monthly research and trends just like this our email list.
